summary: ts-build-optimize executes remote code fetched from an anonymous vercel.app endpoint
details: ts-build-optimize impersonates Microsoft's tslib (README is a near-verbatim copy of microsoft/tslib's runtime-helpers README) but ships none of tslib's helpers. Its primary exported API, buildoptimize(), issues HTTP GETs to https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/<token> and https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/sx/<token> and passes the response body directly to eval(JSON.parse(body)) (index.js:60-63, 71). The destination is an anonymous vercel.app subdomain not associated with any legitimate publisher; content there is mutable and attacker-controlled, so any consumer that invokes the advertised function executes arbitrary attacker-supplied JavaScript in their process. The file also contains an unused iconDomain map referencing cloudflare/fastly/akamai (index.js:14-22) and a setDefaultModule() that references cdnjs / font-awesome — a cover-story unrelated to the real network destination, intended to make casual review pass. The combination of tslib impersonation, anonymous mutable-URL fetch, and eval of the response is a deliberate supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
